repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: Cmdline completion includes deprecated fields.

Solution: Generate deprecated functions into a Lua table from
runtime/doc/deprecated.txt, then use it to filter out deprecated fields when
expanding cmdline completion.

Fix #21660

<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

comment_data.body: can skip this line, only needed with `addDirectoryArg`

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -921,14 +921,38 @@ function vim._expand_pat(pat, env)
 
   local keys = {} --- @type table<string,true>
 
+  --- @param base_parts table<integer, any>
+  local function build_full_path(base_parts, current_key)
+    if #base_parts == 0 then
+      return current_key
+    end
+
+    local path_parts = {}
+    for _, part in ipairs(base_parts) do
+      if type(part) == 'string' then
+        table.insert(path_parts, part)
+      end
+    end
+
+    if #path_parts > 0 then
+      table.insert(path_parts, current_key)
+      return table.concat(path_parts, '.')
+    else
+      return current_key
+    end
+  end
+
   --- @param obj table<any,any>
   local function insert_keys(obj)
+    ---@type boolean, table<string, boolean>?
+    local _, deprecated = pcall(require, 'vim.deprecated.checker')

comment_data.body:
contents of this checker are as follows.
```lua
local deprecated = {
    ['function_name'] = true
    ....
}
return deprecated
```

so the `get`  mean `M.get() return deprecated end` ?

comment_data.body:
I think:

```lua
and not (deprecated and deprecated[build_full_path(parts, k)])
```

is more intuitive here.

Secondly, this is a great feature, and looks essentially ready. Sorry for the necro-review (?) - does anything else remain for this to be pushed over the edge?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -908,14 +908,38 @@ function vim._expand_pat(pat, env)
 
   local keys = {} --- @type table<string,true>
 
+  --- @param base_parts table<integer, any>
+  local function build_full_path(base_parts, current_key)
+    if #base_parts == 0 then
+      return current_key
+    end
+
+    local path_parts = {}
+    for _, part in ipairs(base_parts) do
+      if type(part) == 'string' then
+        table.insert(path_parts, part)
+      end
+    end
+
+    if #path_parts > 0 then
+      table.insert(path_parts, current_key)
+      return table.concat(path_parts, '.')
+    else
+      return current_key
+    end
+  end
+
   --- @param obj table<any,any>
   local function insert_keys(obj)
+    ---@type boolean, table<string, boolean>?
+    local _, deprecated = pcall(require, 'vim._core.deprecated')
     for k, _ in pairs(obj) do
       if
         type(k) == 'string'
         and string.sub(k, 1, string.len(match_part)) == match_part
         and k:match('^[_%w]+$') ~= nil -- filter out invalid identifiers for field, e.g. 'foo#bar'
         and (last_char ~= '.' or string.sub(k, 1, 1) ~= '_') -- don't include private fields after '.'
+        and (not deprecated or not deprecated[build_full_path(parts, k)])

comment_data.body:
as i've said many, many times before, for cases like this, it is extremely easy and high-leverage to give a 1-line example of what this produces.

and similarly, `src/gen/gen_deprecate.lua` does not have any doc...

```suggestion
  --- Example: "..."
  local function build_full_path(base_parts, current_key)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -913,14 +913,38 @@ function vim._expand_pat(pat, env)
 
   local keys = {} --- @type table<string,true>
 
+  --- @param base_parts table<integer, any>
+  local function build_full_path(base_parts, current_key)

comment_data.body:
why get the functions from deprecated.txt, instead of the `@deprecated` annotation (i.e. make this a step in `gen_vimdoc.lua` ?

(I'm not saying it should be done that way, I'm asking why you make this choice. And if you intentionally made the choice, it is useful to say why in the PR description / commit message.)
because I hadn’t carefully read gen_vimdoc before, I wasn’t aware that it could collect deprecated items. I’ve  updated it.

don't join literals
```suggestion
  local out_path = vim.fs.joinpath(base_dir, 'runtime/lua/.../deprecated.lua')
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1188,10 +1164,36 @@ local function gen_target(cfg)
   fp:close()
 end
 
+local function write_deprecated()
+  local out_path = vim.fs.joinpath(base_dir, 'runtime', 'lua', 'vim', '_core', 'deprecated.lua')

comment_data.body:
I think it probably makes sense for this to live in `_meta` (I don't think thats *only* for type definitions? @lewis6991 )

And it has been quite useful to see `.gen.lua` in generated filenames, so let's also do that.

I don't think this needs to be in `_core`, which is compiled-in to the `nvim` binary. If the runtime isn't found (i.e. `require('...deprecated'` fails), then completion should just log an error and continue.

extract to a local function, similar to `insert_keys`. and show a 1-line example
```suggestion
    -- Example: "..."
    path_prefix = #path_parts > 0 and (table.concat(path_parts, '.') .. '.') or ''
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -913,6 +913,23 @@ function vim._expand_pat(pat, env)
 
   local keys = {} --- @type table<string,true>
 
+  local deprecated --- @type table<string, true>
+  do
+    local ok, mod = pcall(require, 'vim._core.deprecated')
+    deprecated = ok and mod or {}
+  end
+
+  local path_prefix --- @type string
+  do
+    local path_parts = {} --- @type string[]
+    for _, part in ipairs(parts) do
+      if type(part) == 'string' then
+        path_parts[#path_parts + 1] = part
+      end
+    end
+    path_prefix = #path_parts > 0 and (table.concat(path_parts, '.') .. '.') or ''

@@ -1188,10 +1164,36 @@ local function gen_target(cfg)
   fp:close()
 end
 
+local function write_deprecated()

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
  it('does not return deprecated symbols', function()
```
also do a `clear{env=...}` with a invalid VIMRUNTIME, and assert that it DOES complete a deprecated item in that case

     )
   end)

+    eq({}, get_completions('vim.api.nvim_buf_add')[1])
+    eq({}, get_completions('vim.lsp.client_is')[1])
+    eq({}, get_completions('vim.regi')[1])
+    eq({}, get_completions('vim.lsp.codelens.cl')[1])
+
+    local completions = get_completions('vim.api.nvim_buf_set_l')[1]
+    eq(true, vim.tbl_contains(completions, 'nvim_buf_set_lines'))
+    local lsp_completions = get_completions('vim.lsp.buf.h')[1]
+    eq(true, vim.tbl_contains(lsp_completions, 'hover'))

comment_data.body: i think we could just write the `vim.inspect` result? that also sorts them.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1188,10 +1164,36 @@ local function gen_target(cfg)
   fp:close()
 end
 
+local function write_deprecated()
+  local out_path = vim.fs.joinpath(base_dir, 'runtime', 'lua', 'vim', '_core', 'deprecated.lua')
+
+  local keys = vim.tbl_keys(deprecated_fns)
+  table.sort(keys)
+
+  local lines = {
+    '-- Generated by gen_vimdoc.lua from @deprecated annotations.',
+    '-- DO NOT EDIT.',
+    '',
+    'return {',
+  }
+  for _, k in ipairs(keys) do
+    lines[#lines + 1] = ("  ['%s'] = true,"):format(k)
+  end
+  lines[#lines + 1] = '}'
+  lines[#lines + 1] = ''

comment_data.body: why do we need to build `t` , can we just write `vim.inspect(deprecated_fns)`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1188,10 +1164,30 @@ local function gen_target(cfg)
   fp:close()
 end
 
+local function gen_deprecated()
+  local out_path = vim.fs.joinpath(base_dir, 'runtime/lua/vim/_meta/deprecated.gen.lua')
+
+  local keys = vim.tbl_keys(deprecated_fns)
+  table.sort(keys)
+  local t = {} --- @type table<any, any>
+  for _, k in ipairs(keys) do
+    t[k] = true
+  end
+
+  local fp = assert(io.open(out_path, 'w'))
+  fp:write('-- Generated by gen_vimdoc.lua from @deprecated annotations.\n')
+  fp:write('-- DO NOT EDIT.\n\n')
+  fp:write('return ' .. vim.inspect(t) .. '\n')

comment_regular_data.body:
Added deprecated checks to make lintdoc, with corresponding output.

```
generated 3 html pages
total errors: 0
invalid tags: {}
redirects: 0

E5113: Lua chunk: ./src/gen/gen_help_html.lua:1348: deprecated.gen.lua is stale, run `make doc` to update
stack traceback:
	[C]: in function 'assert'
	./src/gen/gen_help_html.lua:1348: in function 'check_deprecated'
	scripts/lintdoc.lua:21: in main chunk
FAILED: [code=1] touches/lintdoc /Users/mw/workspace/neovim/build/touches/lintdoc
cd /Users/mw/workspace/neovim && /opt/homebrew/bin/cmake -E touch /Users/mw/workspace/neovim/build/touches/lintdoc && /opt/homebrew/bin/cmake -E env VIMRUNTIME=/Users/mw/workspace/neovim/runtime /Users/mw/workspace/neovim/build/bin/nvim -u NONE -l /Users/mw/workspace/neovim/src/gen/preload.lua /Users/mw/workspace/neovim scripts/lintdoc.lua
ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.
make: *** [lintdoc] Error 1
```

title: feat(lua): cmdline completion ignored deprecated field
